subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Tiles & Board
- **24 desert tiles** — Double-sided sand/location tiles forming a 5x5 grid (with center empty).
- **48 sand markers** — Stackable sand pieces that pile onto tiles.
- **1 storm meter** — Tracks storm intensity from 2 to 6+ (loss threshold).
- **1 storm meter clip** — Marks current storm level.

#### Flying Machine
- **4 flying machine parts** — Propeller, Engine, Navigation Deck, and Crystal.
- **1 flying machine model** — Assembled from collected parts as visual tracker.

#### Cards
- **31 storm cards** — Drive sand movement and Sun Beats Down events.
- **12 equipment cards** — Jetpack, Terrascope, Dune Blaster, Solar Shield, Secret Water Reserve, Time Throttle.
- **6 adventurer cards** — Role cards with unique powers.

#### Player Pieces
- **6 pawns** — One per role color.
- **6 water meter clips** — Track each player's hydration level.
- **6 adventurer cards** — Each with water canteen tracker on the card.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Create the desert** — Shuffle all 24 desert tiles face down and arrange them in a 5x5 grid, leaving the center space empty. This empty space is the storm's eye.
2. **Place sand markers** — Place 1 sand marker on each tile in the 8 spaces directly surrounding the center empty space (the ring of tiles adjacent to the storm eye).
3. **Set the storm meter** — Choose difficulty: Novice (start at 2), Normal (start at 3), Elite (start at 4), or Legendary (start at 5). Place clip on chosen level.
4. **Shuffle storm deck** — Shuffle all 31 storm cards and place face down as a draw pile.
5. **Prepare equipment deck** — Shuffle equipment cards and place face down near the board.
6. **Set machine parts aside** — Place the 4 flying machine parts near the board where all players can reach them.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Role Assignment
- **Shuffle roles** — Shuffle all 6 adventurer cards and deal 1 to each player.
- **Place water clips** — Each player places their water meter clip on their adventurer card at the maximum water level shown for their role.
- **Place pawns** — All players place their pawns on the crash site tile (the center tile that was revealed at the start, which corresponds to the storm eye location).

#### Roles & Starting Water
- **Explorer** — 4 water. Can move and remove sand diagonally.
- **Meteorologist** — 4 water. Can spend actions to draw fewer storm cards or look at upcoming storm cards.
- **Navigator** — 4 water. Can move other players up to 3 spaces as a single action.
- **Water Carrier** — 5 water. Can take water from already-excavated well tiles and give water to adjacent players.
- **Climber** — 3 water. Can move onto and through blocked (2+ sand) tiles and carry one other player along.
- **Archaeologist** — 3 water. Can remove 2 sand markers with a single action.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
1. **All pawns start in the center** — The storm eye is the initial crash site where all players begin.
2. **No tiles are excavated yet** — All 24 tiles start face down (sand side up).
3. **Choose first player** — The thirstiest player goes first (or choose randomly).
4. **Turn flow reminder** — Each turn: take up to 4 actions, then draw storm cards equal to the current storm meter level.
5. **Cooperative discussion** — Players may freely discuss strategy and coordinate actions at all times.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Take up to 4 actions** — Choose from: Move, Remove Sand, Excavate, Pick Up a Part. You may take fewer than 4 actions if desired.
2. **Draw storm cards** — Draw a number of storm cards equal to the current storm meter level (2-6). Resolve each card immediately.
3. **Pass turn** — Play proceeds clockwise to the next player.

#### Storm Card Types
- **Wind cards (directional)** — Show 1, 2, or 3 arrows in a direction. Shift tiles in that direction, moving sand and players with them. The empty storm eye space stays fixed; tiles slide into and out of it.
- **Sun Beats Down** — Every player not on a tunnel tile or under a Solar Shield loses 1 water. No tile shifting occurs.
- **Storm Picks Up** — Advance the storm meter 1 level. Future turns draw more storm cards. No tile shifting occurs.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Move (1 action)
- Move your pawn to an adjacent tile (up, down, left, right — not diagonal unless you are the Explorer).
- **Blocked tiles** — You cannot move onto a tile with 2 or more sand markers unless you are the Climber.
- **Tunnels** — Excavated tunnel tiles allow free movement between any two tunnel entrances as a single move action.

#### Remove Sand (1 action)
- Remove 1 sand marker from a tile adjacent to you or your current tile.
- **Archaeologist** removes 2 sand markers per action instead of 1.
- A tile must have 0 sand markers to be excavated.

#### Excavate (1 action)
- Flip a face-down tile that has 0 sand markers and that you are standing on.
- Reveal the location underneath and resolve its effect immediately.

#### Pick Up a Part (free / 1 action)
- When a machine part's location has been determined (both clue tiles for that part are revealed), the part appears at the intersection. A player on that tile picks it up.
- Picking up a part is a free action if you are already on the tile.

#### Tile Types When Excavated
- **Gear tiles** — Draw equipment cards equal to the number shown (1 or 2).
- **Water tiles** — Players on or adjacent to the tile gain 2 water (up to their max).
- **Tunnel tiles** — Act as shelter from Sun Beats Down and allow tunnel movement.
- **Clue tiles** — Show row or column for one machine part. When both clues for a part are revealed, the part's tile is determined.
- **Mirage tile** — Looks like water but is dry. No water gained.
- **Launch pad** — Where all players must gather with all 4 parts to win.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Sand Storm Movement
- When a wind card is drawn, tiles shift. The storm eye (empty space) effectively moves in the indicated direction, and tiles slide to fill the gap.
- Sand markers move with their tiles. Players move with their tiles.
- After shifting, place 1 sand marker on each tile that just moved into a space adjacent to the new storm eye position (the tiles that slid).
- If the sand supply runs out during this step, the team loses.

#### Machine Part Location
- Each of the 4 parts has 2 clue tiles — one shows the column and one shows the row.
- When both clue tiles for a part are excavated, the part is placed at the tile where the row and column intersect.
- Parts can shift with tile movement before being picked up.

#### Equipment Cards
- **Dune Blaster** — Remove all sand from one tile.
- **Jet Pack** — Move yourself or another player to any unblocked tile.
- **Terrascope** — Secretly look at any face-down tile.
- **Solar Shield** — Protects you from the next Sun Beats Down card.
- **Secret Water Reserve** — Gain 2 water for any player.
- **Time Throttle** — Take 2 extra actions immediately.
- Equipment can be played on your turn or another player's turn (unless stated otherwise). Using equipment does not cost an action.

#### Water Management
- Each player tracks water on their adventurer card. Water cannot exceed a player's maximum.
- Water Carrier can share water with adjacent players as an action.
- Wells only provide water when first excavated (except for the Water Carrier, who can revisit).

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Win Condition
- **Team victory** — All players gather on the launch pad tile with all 4 flying machine parts collected. The team escapes the desert and wins.

#### Loss Conditions
- **Thirst** — Any player's water marker reaches the skull symbol (dehydration). The entire team loses.
- **Buried** — A sand marker must be placed but the sand supply is exhausted. The team loses.
- **Swallowed by the storm** — The storm meter reaches the skull symbol at the top of the track. The team loses.

#### Scoring Model
- **No VP track** — Forbidden Desert is a binary win/loss cooperative game.
- **No final tally** — The game ends immediately when any win or loss condition is triggered.
- **Difficulty scaling** — Players can increase difficulty by starting the storm meter at a higher level.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Clear sand early** — Focus initial actions on removing sand from tiles near the center before the storm buries them deeper.
- **Excavate clue tiles first** — Prioritize uncovering the clue tiles that reveal machine part locations over gear or water tiles.
- **Stay near tunnels** — Once tunnel tiles are excavated, position players near them for efficient movement and Sun Beats Down protection.
- **Spread out strategically** — Don't clump all players together early. Cover more ground to excavate faster, but keep the Water Carrier within sharing range.
- **Watch the storm meter** — If starting at Normal or higher, be aggressive with excavation before Storm Picks Up cards accelerate the pace.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Ignoring sand buildup** — Letting sand pile to 3-4 markers makes tiles extremely expensive to clear. Address sand early.
- **Splitting too far apart** — Players who wander far from the group cannot receive water or equipment help in emergencies.
- **Hoarding equipment** — Equipment cards are powerful and time-sensitive. Waiting for the perfect moment often means losing before you use them.
- **Forgetting Sun Beats Down shelter** — Always note which players are on tunnel tiles. Moving off a tunnel before the storm phase is risky.
- **Neglecting the Water Carrier's ability** — The Water Carrier can revisit excavated wells. Use this repeatedly to keep the team hydrated.
- **Chasing parts before both clues are found** — You cannot pick up a part until both its row and column clue tiles are revealed. Focus on finding clues first.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Sand removal vs. excavation** — Clearing sand is necessary but doesn't progress the win condition. Balance clearing paths with flipping tiles.
- **When to use the Jet Pack** — Save Jet Pack cards for emergency rescue (pulling a thirsty player to water) or the final launch pad gathering.
- **Tunnel positioning** — Decide whether to cluster near tunnels for safety or range out to excavate distant tiles.
- **Water distribution** — Choose whether the Water Carrier spends actions sharing water or exploring. Both are critical.
- **Storm card counting** — Track how many Storm Picks Up and Sun Beats Down cards have appeared. Knowing what's left helps gauge urgency.
- **Launch pad timing** — Once all 4 parts are collected, coordinate movement efficiently to get everyone to the launch pad before the storm overwhelms you.
